{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2242873?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/ashleytolbert", "id": 2242873, "login": "ashleytolbert", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjIyNDI4NzM=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/ashleytolbert", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-50560", "cvss": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary

Netty HTTP/2 max header size handling produces attack similar to HTTP/2 Rapid Reset.

### Details

There is a setting in the http2 specification called `SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE`. According to[ the RFC](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9113.html#name-defined-settings): “This advisory setting informs a peer of the maximum field section size that the sender is prepared to accept, in units of octets.”

When a client sends that setting to Netty, it appears that Netty will behave as follows:

- Read the request
- Proxy the request to the origin
- Attempt to produce a response
- Create an exception while writing the headers for the response

Functionally, this should be similar to the http2 reset attack, but with a different on-the-wire signature.

## Remediation

When speaking with clients, Netty should potentially treat this as “advisory” and ignore it.  It would be best to ignore the SETTINGS_MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE setting from clients (or ignore it when sending to clients). According to the spec, a server does not need to honor this advisory setting, and it appears that other http/2 implementations ignore it when acting as a server.

### Impact

This is a DDoS attack similar to the HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack.

## Credit
Jonathan Looney (Engineering, Netflix)

## Contact
Ashley Tolbert (Security, Netflix) - artolbert@netflix.com", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00302, "percentile": 0.21613}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-15T20:46:56Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-50560"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-12T16:16:32Z", "published_at": "2026-06-15T20:46:56Z", "references": ["https://github.com/netty/netty/security/advisories/GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-50560", "https://github.com/netty/netty/releases/tag/netty-4.1.135.Final", "https://github.com/netty/netty/releases/tag/netty-4.2.15.Final", "https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9113.html#name-defined-settings", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/netty/netty/security-advisories/GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/netty/netty", "summary": "Netty susceptible to HTTP/2 Reset Attack with different on-the-wire signature", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-15T20:46:57Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-563q-j3cm-6jxm", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.2.15.Final", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.netty:netty-codec-http2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.2.0.Final, <= 4.2.14.Final"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.1.135.Final", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.netty:netty-codec-http2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 4.1.134.Final"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Netty susceptible to HTTP/2 Reset Attack with different on-the-wire signature
